task_completion.description: Open the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app is launched and its main interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select Dasatinib as the cancer drug
task_completion.visual_evidence: The drug 'Dasatinib' is typed into a search bar or selected from a list of cancer drugs within the app.
The drug 'Amiodarone' is typed into a search bar or selected from a list of co-medications within the app.

task_completion.description: View the interaction result between the two drugs
task_completion.visual_evidence: A screen within the app showing the interaction between Dasatinib and Amiodarone, displaying a traffic light color code (e.g., AMBER) and a paragraph of interaction details.

task_completion.description: Record the drug names to the result file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor or terminal command writing to '/sdcard/task_result.txt', explicitly including 'Dasatinib' as the first line/item and 'Amiodarone' as the second line/item.

task_completion.description: Record the color code and interaction details to the result file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the text editor or terminal command including the traffic light color code (e.g., AMBER) as the third line/item, and the detailed interaction text as the fourth line/item onwards.

integrity.description: The agent must use the Liverpool Cancer iChart Archive app to find the interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent interacts with the actual app interface rather than using a web browser, external search engine, or hallucinating the result.

integrity.description: The recorded interaction text and color code must match the app's output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interaction text and color code written to the text file visibly match the information displayed in the app's interaction result screen.

integrity.description: The agent must not write the final results before viewing them in the app.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal commands or text editor actions to write '/sdcard/task_result.txt' only occur AFTER the interaction result has been displayed on the screen.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Dasatinib is a cancer drug and Amiodarone is an antiarrhythmic drug. The interaction between them involves QT prolongation and CYP3A4 inhibition, increasing amiodarone concentration and arrhythmia risk. The Liverpool traffic light color system uses AMBER for moderate risk interactions, along with RED, YELLOW, GREEN, and GREY. The required keywords in the interaction text include QT, prolong, CYP, metabolism, concentration, inhibit, risk, and arrhythmia. The app contains real drug interaction data from the Liverpool Drug Interactions Group.
